more quanta produced per unit energy relative to Xe
more quanta produced per unit energy relative to Xe

question: In the context of using the Stopping and Range of Ions in Matter (SRIM) simulation package, how does the production of quanta in LXe compare when considering H recoils versus Xe recoils?

options.A: Xe recoils in LXe using SRIM produce more quanta per unit energy than H recoils, indicating a different physical phenomenon.
options.B: H recoils and Xe recoils cannot be compared using SRIM in terms of quanta production, as they belong to different simulation contexts.
options.C: Both H recoils and Xe recoils in LXe produce an equal amount of quanta per unit energy, demonstrating similar physical phenomena.
options.D: H recoils in LXe using SRIM produce more quanta per unit energy than Xe recoils, indicating a different physical phenomenon.

explanation: The correct answer is A because the paper specifies that H recoils produce more quanta per unit energy than Xe recoils when simulated using the Stopping and Range of Ions in Matter (SRIM) package, thus indicating this as a distinct physical phenomenon.
